steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $f(x) = 98(1.4)^{x-1}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Make a one-row value table
steps.content: The value $k$ means $f(1)$. In Desmos, make one table row with $x_1=[1]$, then evaluate each answer choice at that same input. Since the choices are equivalent forms, they should all output the same value; the goal is to find which one displays that value as the coefficient or base.

steps.title: Find the form where the exponent becomes 0
steps.content: The table shows $f(1)=98$. The coefficient shows $k$ directly only when the exponential factor becomes $1$. The matching choice has $x-1$ in the exponent, so at $x=1$ it gives $98(1.4)^0=98$. The coefficient $98$ is exactly $k$.

steps.title: Eliminate the shifted forms
steps.content: A choice showing the value at $x=-1$, a choice showing the value at $x=0$, and a choice showing the value at $x=2$. Only the matching choice shows $f(1)$ as the coefficient. C is correct.
